notes: Traditional OLTP (e.g. order entry): update-intensive with focus on writes. From cache.
Mix of reads and writes; e.g. SAP R/3. Distinguished from traditional OLTP by read-write balance.
Query-intensive; primarily sequential reads; typical example: data warehousing.
Primarily random reads with light transaction processing; file/database hybrid.
Write once; read once to many times. Low I/O intensity; high retention value.
Interactive design workload; mix of reads and writes on large pieces of data.
Fixed content after creation; ideally immutable; read-only after initial write.
Read-only access of previously-created large bitmap files.
Storage tier positioned between high-performance SCSI/FC and low-cost desktop ATA. Target for nearline and content workloads.
Framework for tiering data across storage classes based on access patterns and value. From cache.
